prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='NaiveMovingAverage', seasonality_champion='ARIMA', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: The validation folds reveal significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.41) and high seasonality variance (0.59), indicating models capture patterns very differently. Early horizon specialists (ONLY_FT_catboost, ONLY_FT_rf) differ from late horizon specialists (CWT_catboost, CWT_rf), suggesting horizon-specific performance. The true series trends upward across all folds, with NaiveMovingAverage best tracking trend while ARIMA excels at seasonality. Given both high disagreement and high seasonality variance, DBA combination is recommended to leverage model diversity while downweighting poor performers.
prompts.skeptic:
Chame build_debate_packet_tool() PRIMEIRO (inputs via context). Depois retorne APENAS JSON (sem markdown) com add_names, remove_names, params_overrides, rationale, changes, when_good.
IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY use candidate names from valid_candidate_names; unknown names hard-stop.
You may only remove/override candidates that are in current_candidate_names (or candidates you are adding).

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "NaiveMovingAverage",
  "seasonality_champion": "ARIMA",
  "overall_champion": "ONLY_FT_rf",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "ONLY_FT_catboost", "late": "CWT_catboost"},
  "tier1_models": ["ONLY_FT_rf", "CWT_catboost", "ONLY_FT_catboost", "ONLY_FT_svr", "ONLY_CWT_rf", "CWT_rf", "FT_rf", "DWT_catboost"],
  "tier2_models": ["rf", "ONLY_DWT_catboost", "ONLY_CWT_catboost", "catboost", "FT_catboost", "ONLY_CWT_svr", "svr", "ARIMA"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 0.4063,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["up", "up", "up"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "The validation folds reveal significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.41) and high seasonality variance (0.59), indicating models capture patterns very differently. Early horizon specialists (ONLY_FT_catboost, ONLY_FT_rf) differ from late horizon specialists (CWT_catboost, CWT_rf), suggesting horizon-specific performance. The true series trends upward across all folds, with NaiveMovingAverage best tracking trend while ARIMA excels at seasonality. Given both high disagreement and high seasonality variance, DBA combination is recommended to leverage model diversity while downweighting poor performers."
}
raw.proposer:
{
  "selected_names": ["dba_combination", "best_per_horizon_by_validation", "topk_mean_per_horizon_k5", "inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35"],
  "params_overrides": {},
  "score_preset": "robust_smape",
  "force_debate": false,
  "debate_margin": 0.02,
  "rationale": "n_unique_winners=5 indicates horizon-specific specialists (early: ONLY_FT_catboost/rf/svr; late: CWT_catboost/rf). relative_spread_mean=1.064 shows high model disagreement, justifying dba_combination as recommended by pattern_analyst_insights. RMSE spread ratio=0.4063 confirms significant performance divergence across models. topk_mean_per_horizon_k5 captures horizon heterogeneity while inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35 provides robust weighting with shrinkage. best_per_horizon_by_validation leverages the 5 unique winners per horizon. Score preset 'robust_smape' chosen because avg SMAPE=0.826 > 0.3 (scale-sensitive series)."
}
raw.skeptic:
{
  "add_names": [],
  "remove_names": [],
  "params_overrides": {},
  "rationale": "Current candidate set has good diversity: 2 selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k5), 1 weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35), 1 baseline (dba_combination). No leakage detected - all candidates use safe anti-leakage design (expanding rolling with train_window=5 for weighted/selection types; baselines never leak). Universe leaderboards show better RMSE/SMAPE/MAPE performers but current set has higher overall scores. Score margin between top2 is 0.0018 indicating close competition. No redundancy issues (only 1 of each type). Diversity rules satisfied with ≥2 different types present.",
  "changes": [],
  "when_good": ""
}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: The validation folds reveal significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.41) and high seasonality variance (0.59), indicating models capture patterns very differently. Early horizon specialists (ONLY_FT_catboost, ONLY_FT_rf) differ from late horizon specialists (CWT_catboost, CWT_rf), suggesting horizon-specific performance. The true series trends upward across all folds, with NaiveMovingAverage best tracking trend while ARIMA excels at seasonality. Given both high disagreement and high seasonality variance, DBA combination is recommended to leverage model diversity while downweighting poor performers.

proposer.rationale: n_unique_winners=5 indicates horizon-specific specialists (early: ONLY_FT_catboost/rf/svr; late: CWT_catboost/rf). relative_spread_mean=1.064 shows high model disagreement, justifying dba_combination as recommended by pattern_analyst_insights. RMSE spread ratio=0.4063 confirms significant performance divergence across models. topk_mean_per_horizon_k5 captures horizon heterogeneity while inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35 provides robust weighting with shrinkage. best_per_horizon_by_validation leverages the 5 unique winners per horizon. Score preset 'robust_smape' chosen because avg SMAPE=0.826 > 0.3 (scale-sensitive series).

skeptic.rationale: Current candidate set has good diversity: 2 selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k5), 1 weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35), 1 baseline (dba_combination). No leakage detected - all candidates use safe anti-leakage design (expanding rolling with train_window=5 for weighted/selection types; baselines never leak). Universe leaderboards show better RMSE/SMAPE/MAPE performers but current set has higher overall scores. Score margin between top2 is 0.0018 indicating close competition. No redundancy issues (only 1 of each type). Diversity rules satisfied with ≥2 different types present.
